package.proposedTitle: Stop Letting AI Choose Your Video Strategy
package.idea: A practical framework for using AI as a research assistant while keeping creator judgment in control.
package.thumbnailConcept: Creator at desk rejecting a chaotic AI suggestion cloud and choosing one clear package card.
package.viewerPromise: Learn how to use AI for faster video ideation without outsourcing taste, positioning, or final judgment.
package.targetViewer: Serious solo creator experimenting with AI tools but worried about generic content.
package.mainRisk: Could become abstract unless grounded in a real example workflow.
shortsIdeas: The 90/10 rule for AI video ideas
Why AI should not pick your final title
One question that exposes generic AI ideas
How to veto an AI package
package.why_this_matters_now: Creators are rapidly adopting AI ideation tools and need a sane way to use them without flattening their voice.
package.why_this_stays_relevant: The principle of separating research speed from editorial judgment remains useful across tool cycles.
package.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: It matches VIDTOOLZ as practical video creation in the AI era with critical tester instincts.
package.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ can show the real workflow, the veto points, and the tradeoffs instead of selling AI hype.
package.audience_demand_rationale: Solo creators are searching for AI workflows but still need confidence that the output will not feel generic.
package.suggested_production_approach: Use a screen-recorded comparison of raw AI suggestions, a structured package scorecard, and a final human-selected package.
